page: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a virtual machine instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 vCPUs allocated on a shared basis, 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, AVX-512 BF16, and AES cryptography. Storage is provided via 1 GB of network-attached storage, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. In single-core stress-ng CPU tests, the instance achieves a top-tier rating in the top 10% with a score of 3688.67, which is mirrored by top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression at 39.96 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.65 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance drops to the bottom 25% with the same score of 3688.67, indicating a lack of multi-core scaling under shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is generally constrained, with read-only bandwidths for both small and large blocks falling into the bottom 25%, and the Passmark memory score of 1368.65 also ranking in the bottom 25%. Large block memory write bandwidth is average at 28.99 GB/sec.

Database and transactional performance are limited, as evidenced by a poor Passmark database operations score of 1033.86 in the bottom 10% and a weak Redis SET operations throughput of 3.96 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is moderate, reaching an average tier of 8.04 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance processes prompt tokens for a small 135M model at 345.62 tokens per second but generates text at an average rate of 106.45 tokens per second. Given the shared CPU model and low-capacity network storage, this instance offers entry-level cost efficiency for workloads that require strong single-threaded burst capability but do not demand sustained multi-core execution or high-speed database transactions. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and lightweight development tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is an x86_64 virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage. The processor operates at 2.0 GHz and features a 32 MB L3 cache alongside AVX-512 instruction support. Performance benchmarks highlight a significant divergence: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the top 10%, whereas multi-core scaling, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25% or lower. Static web serving and small-model text generation achieve average tier results. Lacking a dedicated GPU and high-speed local storage, this budget-friendly, shared-resource instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development environments rather than memory-intensive or database-heavy production workloads.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a shared-CPU virtual server featuring 2 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside constrained multi-core and database capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a shared-CPU instance with 2 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, suited for basic single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server combining strong single-threaded execution with balanced memory for entry-level workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 1 GB of network storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weak multi-core scaling and database transaction throughput
Best suited for basic web serving, utility scripts, and development tasks
